blurb: The walk of love is neither parade nor prison; it is a steady stride whose every step is measured by Christ. These lines urge us to shed the wrinkled garments of former lives and dress each new morning in compassion, humility, formfitting grace. Let the pattern take hold: kindness before complaint, mercy before verdict, so that the watching world may read the gospel simply by watching the way you move through the day.

verses.text: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,
Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:
Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
But ye have not so learned Christ;
